title: Eria dujakorum
intro: Eria dujakorum is a species of flowering orchid native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the reduction of anxiety and stress, as well as improved cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Eria dujakorum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Eria dujakorum is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and is used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Eria dujakorum is classified as a species of orchid, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This genus is part of the tribe Vandeae, which is a subtribe of the subfamily Epidendroideae. Eria dujakorum is further classified into the subfamily Epidendroideae, tribe Vandeae, subtribe Vandeinae, and the genus Eria. The species Eria dujakorum is endemic to India. Eria dujakorum is a rare species found in the Western Ghats region of India.
